options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: ignore_ride_stat_changes
options.description: Disables changes to base ride stats. If enabled, rides will always behave like they do in the base game.

options.name: death_link
options.description:
When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.
This manifests itself in exploding rides. Somebody dying will cause a random ride to crash, and building rides badly will cause others to die. 
 There's a 20 second timer between deathlink events.Fix that coaster quickly!
 This option can be enabled/disabled in game as well by typing !!toggledeathlink in the chat tab of the unlock shop.

options.name: difficulty
options.description:
Choose a difficulty for the randomization. This will make rides have more difficult stat results (If that's enabled), as well as affect
things like the loan interest rate.

options.name: scenario_length
options.description:
Choose how long this game will last. This will affect things such as unlock shop prices and if prerequisites are required before purchase. It's recommended to choose based on how long other worlds in the multi-world take to complete.
Synchronous Short: Around 2 hours to complete.
Synchronous Long: Around 4 hours to complete.
Lengthy: Recommended for Asyncs.
Marathon: Recommended for Asyncs.

options.name: scenario
options.description:
Choose which scenario you'd like to play! Random won't choose scenarios that are unreasonably difficult/tedious.
Future updates will allow custom scenarios.

choices.name: build_your_own_six_flags_belgium
choices.display_name: Build Your Own Six Flags Belgium

choices.name: build_your_own_six_flags_great_adventure
choices.display_name: Build Your Own Six Flags Great Adventure

choices.name: build_your_own_six_flags_holland
choices.display_name: Build Your Own Six Flags Holland

choices.name: build_your_own_six_flags_magic_mountain
choices.display_name: Build Your Own Six Flags Magic Mountain

choices.name: build_your_own_six_flags_park
choices.display_name: Build Your Own Six Flags Park

choices.name: build_your_own_six_flags_over_texas
choices.display_name: Build Your Own Six Flags Over Texas

options.name: randomization_range
options.description:
Influences how spread random values will be (Mostly this affects the excitement, intensity, and nausea ratings). The more extreme this value,
the more extreme the difficulty will swing, with easy becoming much easier and hard becoming much more difficult.

options.name: stat_rerolls
options.description: How often to re-randomize the stats for ride types. Build the Theme Park of Theseus!

options.name: randomize_park_values
options.description: Randomizes values such as starting cash, starting bank loan amount, and the max bank loan

options.name: preferred_intensity
options.description:
Choose a preferred intensity for your guests. Less intense will limit guests to a maximum of 4 intensity, and more intense will limit guests
to a minimum of 8 intensity in most circumstances. Normal is highly recommended for most players! Seriously, unless you really know what you 
are doing and want the (likely very tedious) challenge, you shouldn't change this.

options.name: guest_objective
options.description: Choose how many guests are required to win the scenario

options.name: park_value_objective
options.description: If enabled, choose what park value (In USD) is required to win the scenario.

options.name: roller_coaster_objective
options.description: If enabled, choose how many coasters, and what prerequisites they need to beat the scenario.

options.name: roller_coaster_excitement
options.description: Select the minimum excitement 😀 for a coaster to count towards your objective. 0 will disable a minimum excitement rating.

options.name: roller_coaster_intensity
options.description: Select the minimum intensity 😬 for a coaster to count towards your objective. 0 will disable a minimum intensity rating.

options.name: roller_coaster_nausea
options.description: Select the minimum nausea 🤢 for a coaster to count towards your objective. 0 will disable a minimum nausea rating.

options.name: required_unique_rides
options.description:
Requires specific rides to be built before scenario completion is awarded. These will tend to appear in the later half of the game.
It's highly recommended to have this set to at least 1!

options.name: unique_rides_placement
options.description: No-op; still here to prevent old YAMLs from breaking

options.name: park_rating_objective
options.description: If enabled, choose the minimum park rating needed to beat the scenario.

options.name: pay_off_loan
options.description: Require Loan to be paid off before scenario completion is awarded. Multiple objectives can be enabled!

options.name: monopoly_mode
options.description: Monopoly Mode is a new objective type. Every unowned tile will be set to purchasable (Or purchasable construction rights for any unowned tile with a grounded path. Elevated paths will not be purchasable). To complete the objective, all tiles on the map must be purchased. Multiple Objectives can be enabled!

options.name: fireworks
options.description: Have an explosive firework display on victory! Strongly discouraged if you intend to keep playing after victory or if you expect to have a huge park.

options.name: difficult_guest_generation
options.description: Makes guests harder to generate. Unlockable puts an item in generation that disables the rule when found.

options.name: difficult_park_rating
options.description: Makes park rating harder to improve. Unlockable puts an item in generation that disables the rule when found.

options.name: forbid_high_construction
options.description: Limits building to tree height. Unlockable puts an item in generation that disables the rule when found.

options.name: forbid_landscape_changes
options.description: Forbids the landscape from being altered. Unlockable puts an item in generation that disables the rule when found.

options.name: forbid_marketing_campaigns
options.description: Forbids marketing campaigns. Unlockable puts an item in generation that disables the rule when found.

options.name: forbid_tree_removal
options.description: Forbids removing trees. Unlockable puts an item in generation that disables the rule when found.

options.name: shop_minimum_excitement
options.description:
If the shop determines you need a ride with a minimum excitement, this value will be the lowest it can ask for.
If this value is higher than the maximum, the generator will assume it is a mistake and set it to 0.

options.name: shop_maximum_excitement
options.description: If the shop determines you need a ride with a minimum excitement, this value will be the highest it can ask for.

options.name: shop_minimum_intensity
options.description:
If the shop determines you need a ride with a minimum intensity, this value will be the lowest it can ask for.
If this value is higher than the maximum, the generator will assume it is a mistake and set it to 0.

options.name: shop_maximum_intensity
options.description: If the shop determines you need a ride with a minimum intensity, this value will be the highest it can ask for.

options.name: shop_minimum_nausea
options.description:
If the shop determines you need a ride with a minimum nausea, this value will be the lowest it can ask for.
If this value is higher than the maximum, the generator will assume it is a mistake and set it to 0.

options.name: shop_maximum_nausea
options.description: If the shop determines you need a ride with a minimum nausea, this value will be the highest it can ask for.

options.name: shop_minimum_length
options.description:
If the shop determines you need a ride with a minimum length (In Meters), this value in meters will 
be the lowest it can ask for. If this value is higher than the maximum, the generator will assume it is a 
mistake and set it to 0.

options.name: shop_maximum_length
options.description:
If the shop determines you need a ride with a minimum length (In Meters), 
this value will be the highest it can ask for.

options.name: shop_minimum_total_customers
options.description:
If the shop determines you need a ride with a minimum total number of customers, this value will be the 
lowest it can ask for. If this value is higher than the maximum, the generator will assume it is a mistake and 
set it to 0.

options.name: shop_maximum_total_customers
options.description:
If the shop determines you need a ride with a minimum total number of customers, this value will be the 
highest it can ask for.

options.name: balance_guest_counts
options.description:
Attempts to balance the minimum guest requirements to the ride they're attached to. Low throughput rides
like Spiral Slides will tend towards the minimum, while high throughput rides like roller coasters will 
tend towards the maximum.

options.name: awards
options.description:
Choose what types (if any) of awards will have checks behind them. 
Negative awards will always reward a trap item, even if they're otherwise disabled!

All Awards will have a check behind every award in the game. 

Positive will only place a check behind awards with positive attributes.

None will not place checks behind awards.

options.name: exclude_safest_park
options.description: Exclude the Safest Park Award from having a check. This may be useful depending on deathlink settings.

options.name: visibility
options.description:
Choose how much the unlock shop displays. 

"Nothing" tells you nothing about the item you'll purchase. 

Progression tells you what class of item (Normal, Progression, Useful Trap) you're sending.

Recipient tells you who will receive the item, but not what they'll receive. 

Progression Recipient tells you both the class of item and who receives it.

Full tells you what you're buying and who receives it.

options.name: filler
options.description: How many extra filler items to add to the mix as a percentage. This will mostly consist of Cash Bonuses.

options.name: include_atm
options.description: Includes an ATM in the item list, regardless of whether it normally appears in the chosen scenario.

options.name: include_first_aid
options.description: Includes a First Aid Room in the item list, regardless of whether it normally appears in the chosen scenario.

options.name: all_rides_and_scenery_base
options.display_name: Include All Rides and Scenery (Base Game)
options.description: Adds every ride and scenery pack in the base game to the item pool. This will make for a significantly longer scenario.

options.name: all_rides_and_scenery_expansion
options.display_name: Include All Rides and Scenery (Expansion Packs)
options.description:
Adds every ride and scenery pack in the RCT2 Expansion Packs to the item pool. If this is true the base game rides and 
scenery will also be included. This will make for a significantly longer scenario.

options.name: skips
options.description:
By default, every game starts with a single skip to ignore a shop requirement. This will add additional skips to 
be found in the item pool.

options.name: furry_convention_traps
options.description: When found, instantly hosts a furry convention in your park! Adding traps will increase the total number of items in the world.

options.name: bathroom_traps
options.description: When found, instantly maxes out the bathroom stat of every guest! Adding traps will increase the total number of items in the world.

options.name: spam_traps
options.description: When found, spams ads all over the screen! Adding traps will increase the total number of items in the world.

options.name: loan_shark_traps
options.description: When found, increases your loan! Adding traps will increase the total number of items in the world.

options.name: food_poisioning_traps
options.description: When found, gives everybody with food in the park Norovirus! Adding traps will increase the total number of items in the world.

options.name: include_gamespeed_items
options.description: If included, the ability to use the speed toggle will be restricted behind an item. 4 items total will be added, each progressively unlocking a faster speed.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.description: Prevent these locations from having an important item.

options.name: priority_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an unimportant item.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.
